summary: Problem 2 concerns the optimal aspect ratio for piecewise-linear Möbius band embeddings in R^3, with the correct infimum value of sqrt(3) (the Halpern–Weaver constant) identified. However, the current proof attempt is only ~10% complete, having stated the answer without establishing the core mathematical argument. Substantial work remains on both the lower bound and the construction achieving the infimum.
problems: The proof is only ~10% complete: the correct answer (inf = sqrt(3)) is asserted but the supporting argument is essentially absent.
Missing lower bound proof: no rigorous demonstration that no embedding achieves an aspect ratio below sqrt(3).
Missing construction/approximating sequence showing the infimum sqrt(3) is approached (and clarifying it is not attained).
The piecewise-linear setting may require specific handling distinct from the smooth Halpern–Weaver result, which is not yet addressed.
highlights: The final answer sqrt(3) is correctly identified as the Halpern–Weaver constant.
The problem is connected to a known result, giving a clear target and reference framework for the full proof.

suggested_todos.title: Write the lower bound argument proving no PL Möbius embedding has aspect ratio below sqrt(3)
Construct an explicit sequence of PL embeddings whose aspect ratios converge to sqrt(3)
Clarify and justify whether the infimum is attained in the PL setting versus only approached
Reconcile the PL construction with the smooth Halpern–Weaver result and cite the relevant literature
